mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, and PDGFR, reducing proliferation of FLT3‑mutant AML cells
additional_notes: Approved for renal cell carcinoma (RCC) and gastrointestinal stromal tumors (GIST); reported antiproliferative IC50 ~20–200 nM in FLT3‑ITD cells

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases, ABL, and KIT, impairing AML cell proliferation and leukemic stem cell functions
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL; AML cell line IC50 often ~10–100 nM for viability

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT, and PDGFR, affecting KIT/FLT3‑driven AML signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML; AML cell line IC50 typically ~0.1–3 µM

mechanism: Blocks JAK‑STAT signaling, reducing growth of JAK‑STAT–dependent AML cells and showing synergy with FLT3 inhibitors
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera (PV); leukemia IC50 ~0.2–5 µM

mechanism: Primarily inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, reducing survival of cytokine/JAK‑dependent leukemia cells
additional_notes: Approved for rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and psoriatic arthritis (PsA); leukemia models often show 1–10 µM IC50

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK), disrupting prosurvival signaling pathways that support AML cell survival and stemness in some subtypes
additional_notes: Approved for CLL and mantle cell lymphoma (MCL); AML cell lines often show ~2–10 µM IC50 and synergy with other agents

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic proteins BCL‑2, BCL‑XL, and BCL‑w, promoting apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; strong preclinical AML activity including MOLM‑13 with ~10–200 nM IC50

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and NF‑κB; in the presence of copper, inhibits the proteasome and multiple oncogenic pathways, killing AML stem/progenitor cells
additional_notes: Approved for alcohol dependence; AML IC50 often 0.1–2 µM with physiologic copper; suggested to co‑treat with ~1–2 µM Cu²⁺ (e.g., CuCl₂)

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, preferentially targeting OXPHOS‑dependent leukemia stem cells and AML blasts
additional_notes: Approved for type 2 diabetes; leukemia in vitro IC50 often ~1–10 mM

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibitor at pharmacologic levels, inducing differentiation and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved for epilepsy and bipolar disorder; used clinically in AML combination trials; AML IC50 ~0.5–5 mM

mechanism: Potent inhibition of multiple HDAC isoforms, causing strong cytotoxicity and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; AML IC50 generally ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Cereblon‑mediated degradation of IKZF1/3 and immunomodulatory actions contributing to anti‑leukemic effects in selected AML subsets
additional_notes: Used for multiple myeloma; grouped with lenalidomide in the text; AML IC50 often ~5–50 µM

mechanism: Blocks autophagosome–lysosome fusion, inhibiting autophagy on which some AML, especially FLT3‑ITD cells, may depend
additional_notes: Repurposed as an autophagy inhibitor; AML viability IC50 often ~5–20 µM; hydroxychloroquine is a related alternative

mechanism: Inhibits the mevalonate pathway, reducing prenylation and membrane localization of RAS and other small GTPases, leading to antiproliferative effects in AML
additional_notes: Approved for hyperlipidemia; AML cell lines typically show ~1–10 µM IC50

mechanism: Generates reactive oxygen species and interferes with iron metabolism, causing cytotoxicity in AML cells including FLT3‑ITD subsets
additional_notes: Antimalarial with multiple anticancer reports; AML IC50 commonly ~1–10 µM

mechanism: Acts as a mitochondrial uncoupler and inhibits Wnt/β‑catenin, STAT3, and NF‑κB signaling, producing strong antiproliferative effects in AML
additional_notes: Approved anti‑helminthic; AML cell lines typically show ~0.2–2 µM IC50
